extraction.one_line_summary: CARUSO attacked with Bilbao/Bardet on Splugen descent, gained 24 seconds as MARTINEZ's pace-making isolated BERNAL but controlled the gap perfectly.
tactical_patterns: Team DSM caught break on Bernardino descent then sheltered BARDET while BILBAO/CARUSO attacked, forcing INEOS to chase understaffed.
MARTINEZ's pace on final climb was calibrated to drop YATES/ALMEIDA while preserving BERNAL's advantage over CARUSO's gap.
CARUSO attacked when only 2 INEOS domestiques remained after Splugen, exploiting numerical disadvantage before final climb started.

rider_signals.MARTINEZ Daniel: positive: Colombian's pace on final climb dropped YATES and ALMEIDA consecutively, showing he's evolved beyond pure domestique role
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: First-ever GT stage win after career as domestique suggests late-race fatigue neutralizes typical power差gaps
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: negative: Fourth place on third consecutive mountain stage reveals ceiling at altitude against lighter climbers
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Dropped by MARTINEZ's pace suggests declining form in third week, vulnerable even on moderate gradients
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: Sat on CARUSO's wheel entire Splugen attack but couldn't contest stage, prioritizing GC over opportunism
